World.title: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, leaving four critically hurt
World.desc: The accident happened early on Thursday at a level crossing north of Copenhagen.

World.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
World.desc: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

World.title: US Navy chief leaving post 'effective immediately', Pentagon says
World.desc: Reports say Phelan, whose role was largely administrative, left amid tension over US shipbuilding.

World.title: Girl, 14, found in singer D4vd's car died of 'multiple injuries'
World.desc: The medical examiner said the 14-year-old's cause of death was determined months ago but was blocked from release.

World.title: Zambia's government takes possession of ex-president's body in repatriation row
World.desc: Zambia authorities and Edgar Lungu's family are in a dispute over where former leader's remains should be buried.

World.title: Pope Leo criticises Equatorial Guinea prisons as he winds up Africa tour
World.desc: The Pope gives a frank and passionate speech, the likes of which have characterised his tour of Africa.

Technology.title: Xbox cuts prices for Game Pass but ends day-one Call of Duty access
Technology.desc: New Call of Duty games will be added to the subscription service "about a year" after release, Microsoft said.

Technology.title: Anthropic investigating claim of unauthorised access to Mythos AI tool
Technology.desc: The AI company has said the model is too dangerous to release publicly because of its hacking capabilities.

Technology.title: UK gaming icon Peter Molyneux on AI, his final creation and a changing industry
Technology.desc: The creator of iconic series such as Fable says Masters of Albion will be the last game he makes.

Technology.title: Meta to track workers' clicks and keystrokes to train AI
Technology.desc: The firm will take data from the way employees work for its artificial intelligence models.

Technology.title: Ofcom investigating Telegram over child sexual abuse material concerns
Technology.desc: The popular messaging service told the BBC in a statement it "categorically denies Ofcom's accusations".

Technology.title: How will Apple change under 'product guy' John Ternus?
Technology.desc: How Ternus responds to challenges facing Apple will go a long way to deciding the tech giant's future, writes Zoe Kleinman.

Science.title: Ban 'forever chemicals' in uniforms and frying pans, MPs urge
Science.desc: School uniforms and non-stick pans are some of the everyday products that should stop using chemicals called PFAS, MPs say.

Science.title: Electricity bills targeted in planned shakeup to energy pricing
Science.desc: The war in the Middle East has brought renewed attention to Britain's vulnerability to energy price shocks.

Science.title: Artemis II crew: 'We left as friends - we came back as best friends'
Science.desc: The four crew members gave their first press conference since they splashed down nearly a week ago, and emphasised hope and unity.

Science.title: Butterfly numbers are dropping but here are five species you may see more of
Science.desc: A warming climate has helped some to flourish, researchers say, but the outlook is troubling.

Science.title: Golden eagles' return to English skies gets government backing
Science.desc: The birds could be reintroduced as early as next year following a £1m injection from the government.

Science.title: From blast off to splashdown: My days following Nasa's historic mission to the Moon
Science.desc: BBC Science Editor Rebecca Morelle reflects on how it felt to watch history being made.
